title: Effects of Mindfulness Yoga vs Stretching and Resistance Training Exercises on Anxiety and Depression for People With Parkinson Disease: A Randomized Clinical Trial
headline: Effects of Mindfulness Yoga vs Stretching and Resistance Training Exercises on Anxiety and Depression for Peop
summary: A randomized controlled trial of yoga examined emotional wellbeing. Among patients with mild-to-moderate PD, the mindfulness yoga program was found to be as effective as SRTE in improving motor dysfunction and mobility, with the additional benefits of a reduction in anxiety and depressive symptoms and an increase in spiritual well-being and HRQOL.
keyFindings: Among patients with mild-to-moderate PD, the mindfulness yoga program was found to be as effective as SRTE in improving motor dysfunction and mobility, with the additional benefits of a reduction in anxiety and depressive symptoms and an increase in spiritual well-being and HRQOL.
limitations: The participants may not represent the general population, which limits how far the findings generalize.
Single-trial result; requires replication, and self-reported outcomes may inflate apparent effects.

The practice studied (yoga) is not the Miracle of Mind practice, so its findings don't transfer directly — though the shared directed-attention component may make some transfer plausible (see “Why this matters for the 7-minute practice” below).
simplifiedTitle: Effects of Mindfulness Yoga vs Stretching and Resistance Training Exercises on Anxiety and Depression for Peop
aiSnippet: RCT, JAMA neurology (2019). Among patients with mild-to-moderate PD, the mindfulness yoga program was found to be as effective as SRTE in improving motor dysfunction and mobility, with the additional benefits of a reduction in anxiety and depressive symptoms and an increase in spiritual well-being and HRQOL.
whyItMatters: MoM shares directed attention (the focused-attention component) with the practice studied here. Because the benefit is linked to that mechanism rather than to any one method, it's reasonable to expect it may extend to MoM as well — though MoM hasn't been studied directly for this outcome.
